data.title: Stocks in India Hit 5-week Low
data.description:
SENSEX decreased to a 5-week low of 80428.00 Index Points.

Over the past 4 weeks, SENSEX gained 0.67%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 6.35%.

data.title: CHC Healthcare Sets Conversion Price, Premium Rate of Bonds
data.description:
CHC Healthcare Group set the conversion price and premium rate for its fifth domestic issue of unsecured convertible corporate bonds.

The company fixed the conversion price at NT$48.4 per share with a premium rate of 105.2%, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Eni Unit Opens New Logistics Base in Congo
data.description:
Italian energy group Eni's (ENI.MI) Congo-based unit inaugurated a logistics platform at the Saipem base to support its liquefied natural gas project and other infrastructure developments in the country.

Eni Congo's 190,000 square-meter Yasika logistics base has a quay, jetty, office space, and mud and cement treatment units for drilling activities, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on June 13
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Friday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

POLITICS

Italy's Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani on Thursday said his country needs at least 10 years to raise defence spending and comply with new targets due to be agreed by NATO, adding that a deal on them could soon be reached.

ECONOMY

ISTAT releases April foreign trade data (0800 GMT).

The Italian government on Thursday earmarked 200 million euros to keep afloat the steel company Acciaierie d'Italia (ADI), formerly known as Ilva, while negotiations to sell it are ongoing, the industry minister said.

COMPANIES

STELLANTIS

U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday warned he may soon hike auto tariffs, arguing that could prod automakers to speed U.S. investments.

PIRELLI

The tyremaker investors on Thursday approved the group's earnings report for last year despite the opposition of its largest shareholder, the Chinese state-controlled group Sinochem , the company said.

GENERALI , MEDIOBANCA , BANCA GENERALI

The insurer said on Thursday it had begun evaluating Mediobanca's bid for Generali's unit Banca Generali and the potential subsequent partnership with Mediobanca in the wealth management sector.

ENI

The energy group is closely monitoring the security situation in Iraq, a spokesperson said on Thursday after the United States decided to evacuate its embassy in the country.

ENERGY

Italiana Petroli, which is currently in talks with three suitors interested in buying the oil refiner, is expected to take a decision on the potential 3 billion euro deal at the end of june, two sources said on Thursday.

DIARY

Rome, Bank of Italy holds first 2025 Macroprudential Policies Committee meeting, chaired by Bank of Italy Governor Fabio Panetta, with market regulator CONSOB Chairman Paolo Savona, insurance supervisor IVASS President Luigi Federico Signorini, Treasury Director General Riccardo Barbieri (1400 GMT).

Rapallo, business lobby Confindustria's Youth Group starts its annual meeting (1230 GMT); ends on June 14. On Friday expected attendees include Infrastructure and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini, Industry Minister Adolfo Urso, Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani.

((Milan newsroom, e-mail: reutersitaly@thomsonreuters.com))

data.title: Global nuclear arms spending up 11% in 2024, campaign group says
data.description:
Spending on nuclear weapons by the world's nine nuclear-armed nations rose by 11% in 2024, a report by the International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons said on Friday.

The $10 billion annual increase to $100.2 billion went towards modernising and in some cases expanding nuclear arsenals, according to ICAN, a global civil society coalition that seeks the total elimination of atomic weapons.

"Nuclear-armed countries could have paid the United Nations' budget 28 times with what they spent to build and maintain nuclear weapons in 2024," the report said.

The U.S. recorded the largest annual increase in nuclear spending in 2024, rising by $5.3 billion, the report said. Its total expenditure of $56.8 billion exceeded the combined spending of all other nuclear-armed states, it said.

China spent $12.5 billion, followed by Britain at $10.4 billion, which was an increase of $2.2 billion, ICAN said.

It said the other nuclear-armed states were France, India, Israel, North Korea, Pakistan and Russia.

“In terms of kind of the increase in spending in the UK and France, I think we certainly have seen, at least in the rhetoric of political leaders, a reference to the ongoing war in Ukraine, to the tensions, and that could be playing a role,” Alicia Sanders-Zakre, a policy and research coordinator at ICAN, told reporters at a briefing in Geneva.

Britain and other allies in NATO now regard Russia as the main security threat to Europe and some have rolled out plans to devote a higher percentage of GDP to defence spending.

However, Sanders-Zakre said the increase in nuclear expenditure has been more driven by the costs of servicing long-term contracts and the growing expense of developing nuclear delivery systems than by current security concerns.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Financial Services Headlines at 12 AM ET: Chime Financial Is the Latest IPO to Soar in Debut | Why ...
data.description:
Chime Financial Is the Latest IPO to Soar in Debut

Chime Financial's stock jumped in its stock-market debut Thursday, the latest sign the U.S. initial public offering market is awakening.

----

Why Larry Fink Sees BlackRock Shares, Income Doubling by 2030

The world's largest asset manager plans to raise $400 billion from clients by 2030 for its funds that invest in private equity, private credit, real estate, infrastructure and other so-called alternatives to public stocks and bonds.

----

Norway Wealth Fund Finds TD Bank an 'Unacceptable Risk'

Norway's $1.9 trillion sovereign wealth fund has placed Toronto-Dominion Bank under observation for a period of four years over financial crime concerns.

----

Coatue Management's Philippe Laffont Sees 2025 as 'Year of the Privates'

The investor points to artificial intelligence and other disruptive technologies as key areas for future investment.

----

Professional Investors Worry They're Falling Behind in Alts, Survey Finds

More than 90% of institutional investors and advisors say they plan to increase their exposure to private markets in the next two years.

----

More Financial Advisers Are Outsourcing Investment Decisions

Assets following model portfolios rose to a record $8 trillion in April.

----

Chime IPO: Stock Soars in Nasdaq Debut, Valuing It at Nearly $14 Billion

Chime, a digital bank popular with younger customers, is the latest tech firm to go public. The stock will begin trading Thursday under the ticker symbol CHYM.

----

The Great ETF Fee War Is Over. You Can Still Get Burned.

Last year, the average asset-weighted fee for index funds stood at 0.11%, essentially flat with 2023. A decade ago, the average fee was 0.19%.

----

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on pay platforms, Deutsche Bank, NatWest and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

----

PwC Overhauls U.S. Advisory Arm, Boosts Hiring to Offer More Industry-Specific Services

PricewaterhouseCoopers is overhauling the structure of its U.S. advisory business and hiring for thousands of roles as it sees recovering demand for such services.

----

World Bank Sees U.S. Growth Rate Halving as Tariffs Slow Global Economy

The world's largest economy is expected to grow by just 1.4% in 2025, a sharp deceleration from the 2.8% expansion recorded in 2024.

----

Moody's Sounds Alarm on Private Funds for Individuals

Chasing retail money could pose risks to investors, private funds and the financial system, Moody's said.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 12 AM ET: This crucial corner of the energy market is at high risk from an all-out war between Israel and Iran - and it's not oil | Oil ...
data.description:
This crucial corner of the energy market is at high risk from an all-out war between Israel and Iran - and it's not oil

An all-out war between Israel and Iran would pose a serious threat to eastern Mediterranean natural-gas fields - and risk spilling into Europe's liquefied natural gas supply.

----

Oil Stages Timid Pullback As Geopolitical Risk Remains

Oil futures held onto most of yesterday's gains despite some early profit-taking amid high Middle East risk perception.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Storage Improves, but Long-Term Challenges Remain

U.S. natural-gas storage levels have improved since a colder-than-expected winter drained inventories, but rapid growth in liquefied natural-gas exports is causing concerns that the demand will result in higher prices later this year - and beyond.

----

Sunnova Bankruptcy Loan and Sales Approved, Hurdles Remain

The rooftop-solar installation company received approval for an interim loan and to sell some assets to finance its bankruptcy proceedings

----

U.S. Natural Gas Mixed With Focus on Weather Outlook

U.S. natural gas futures put in a mixed performance, supported by a warmer weather outlook and a pickup in LNG feedgas flows. Nymex gas for July delivery settled down, while August and September contracts edged up.

----

Iran Says It Will Open Secret New Enrichment Site After U.N. Atomic Agency Censure

Tehran's move casts a fresh shadow over nuclear talks with Washington, while President Trump says an Israeli strike "could very well happen."

----

Saudi Arabia Is Grabbing Oil-Market Share, but It Can Open the Tap Only So Far

Riyadh wants to squeeze U.S. frackers, although an all-out price war would be costly.

----

EPA to Repeal Air-Pollution Standards on Power Plants

The proposals would roll back Biden-era climate rules and Obama-era mercury limits.

----

How Amazon's Nuclear Deal Could Solve a Touchy Political Problem

The data-center agreement with Talen Energy could help ease fears of rising electricity prices for homeowners.

----

U.S. Moves to Withdraw Some Middle East Personnel as Iran Tensions Rise

The State Department ordered all nonessential staff to leave the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad and authorized the departure of nonessential staff and families from Bahrain and Kuwait.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insights on oil futures, BP, Medco Energi Internasional, and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

U.S. Crude-Oil Stockpiles Down More Than Expected

U.S. crude-oil inventories fell last week by 3.6 million barrels, surpassing an expected decline of 1.6 million-barrels.

----

NRG Energy Used This Accounting Trick to Make Its Earnings Look Less Volatile

The company's interpretation of accounting rules allows it to mask swings in its derivative contracts.

----

Kimmeridge Cements Plan to Build Integrated Natural-Gas Company

Backing from sovereign-wealth investor Mubadala helped open the door for a U.S. wellhead-to-water business.

data.title: Oil India Shares Rise 3.0%, Oil & Natural Gas Shares Gain 1.6%
ICRA to Acquire 100% Stake in Fintellix India for $26 million
data.description:
Ratings and research firm ICRA has agreed to acquire a 100% stake in Fintellix India for $26 million, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Fintellix is a product-led company specializing in risk, supervisory, and data analytics solutions, offered on its data platform.

The acquisition will boost ICRA Group's credit risk assessment and monitoring tool portfolio by incorporating risk reporting capabilities into its current offerings, the filing said.

data.title: Dimerix Meets Around 70% of Recruitment Target for Phase Three Kidney Disease Trial; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Dimerix said that a total of 200 adult patients were randomized and dosed in its Action3 phase three clinical trial in patients with focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, representing around 70% of the total number of patients to be dosed in the two-year study, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm is targeting a total recruitment of around 286 adult patients. Full recruitment of patients into the trial is on track for the second half.

The trial is seeking to evaluate the efficacy and safety of the firm's DMX200 treatment candidate in patients with focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, a kidney disease, the filing said. The participants are receiving a stable dose of an angiotensin II receptor blocker, and once this dose is stable, patients will be randomized to receive either DMX200 or a placebo.

The trial is designed to capture evidence of kidney function and high levels of protein in the urine, which will be used as evidence to support marketing approval. The company has activated over 190 clinical sites in 22 countries.

The company's shares fell over 5% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Crédit Agricole Unit Buys Majority Stake in France's Comwatt
data.description:
Crédit Agricole's subsidiary Crédit Agricole Transitions & Énergies purchased a majority stake in Comwatt, a France-based supplier of photovoltaic equipment.

The banking group said Thursday the deal will not have a significant impact on its common equity tier 1 ratio.

data.title: Western Region Gold to Acquire Xinjiang Meisheng Mining for 1.66 Billion Yuan; Shares Soar 10%
data.description:
Western Region Gold plans to acquire Xinjiang Meisheng Mining for 1.66 billion yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

Xinjiang Meisheng will be bought from its controlling shareholder, Xinjiang Non-ferrous Metals Industry (Group).

Xinjiang Meisheng has a mine that is still under development, with trial operations beginning in late May, the Chinese gold smelting company announced.

The company's shares soared 10% in recent trade.

data.title: Guoyuan Securities Offering Up to 1.5 Billion Yuan Bonds
data.description:
Guoyuan Securities is offering up to 1.5 billion yuan in three-year, unsecured bonds.

Proceeds from the bonds, due June 17, 2028, will be used for repaying medium- and long-term bonds, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Western Securities serves as underwriter.

Shares of the securities brokerage were down nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: SNG: CBE appoints DPI as manager of Nclude FinTech Fund
data.description:
Staff Writer

Arab Finance: The Central Bank of Egypt (CBE) has approved the request from key limited partners of the Nclude FinTech Fund to name Development Partners International (DPI) as the new fund manager, according to a press release.

The appointment aligns with the ongoing efforts to support companies operating in the fintech sector, accelerating digital transformation and promoting financial inclusion.

This move aims to attract new foreign investments in the fintech landscape, backing the fund's objectives to become the largest FinTech-focused fund across the region, with a targeted capital of $150 million.

The fund played an effective role in drawing foreign investments to this sector, where every $1 invested by Nclude in startups operating in the Egyptian market has leveraged an additional $5 from foreign investors into those startups.

Rami Aboulnaga, Deputy Governor of the CBE, commented: "The participation of Egyptian banks in the “Nclude” fund reflects the banking sector's strong commitment to investing in emerging FinTech trends and keeping pace with global trends in these promising sectors, especially by fostering a supportive environment for FinTech entrepreneurs to launch their solutions in Egypt, contributing to achieve targeted financial inclusion rates."

The launch was backed by Egypt’s leading banks, including Banque Misr, the National Bank of Egypt (NBE), and Banque du Caire.

Institutional investors like the Egyptian Banks Company for Technological Advancement (EBC), e-Finance Investment Group, and Mastercard also participated in the ceremony.

data.title: Cordlife Shareholders Advised to Reject Medeze's Partial Offer
data.description:
Novus Corporate Finance, the independent financial adviser hired by Cordlife to assess a partial offer from Medeze Group, recommended that shareholders reject the offer.

Novus Corporate Finance described the offer as "not fair" and "not reasonable" as it was lower than the volume-weighted average price of Cordlife's shares, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Medeze is offering to purchase a 10% stake in Cordlife at SG$0.25 per share.

data.title: South Korea's ICT Exports Jump 9.6% in May
data.description:
South Korea's information and communication technology exports rose 9.6% to $20.9 billion in May from a year earlier, the Ministry of Science and ICT said in a Thursday release.

Imports increased 0.5% to $11.5 billion, resulting in a trade surplus of $9.4 billion, the release said.

Exports rose for semiconductors, mobile phones, computers, and communication devices, while display exports declined. Semiconductor gains were driven by rebounding chip prices and strong demand for high-end memory, while mobile and computer exports benefited from increased smartphone and solid-state drives demand, it said.

Communication device exports grew on U.S. auto and Indian 5G demand, but displays declined due to global demand weakness and trade uncertainty, the release said.

data.title: Indian oil producers rise, refiners slip as Middle East escalation stokes supply fears
data.description:
** Shares of Indian oil producers jump, refiners drop

** ONGC and Oil India rise 2% and 3%, respectively

** State-run Indian Oil , BPCL , HPCL fall 3%, 3.3% and 3.5%, respectively

** Private refiner Reliance Industries slips 1.2%

** Crude prices soar over 10% after Israel said it struck Iran, dramatically escalating tensions in the Middle East and raising worries about disrupted oil supplies

** Rise in crude prices fetches upstream cos higher selling prices of oil and is a potential positive for them

** It is negative for refiners that process the raw material

** Nifty oil and gas (.NIFOILGAS) sub-index down 1.35% on the day, while benchmark Nifty 50 down 1.2%

data.title: The Keg Royalties Income Fund announces June 2025 cash distribution
data.description:
VANCOUVER, British Columbia, June 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The Keg Royalties Income Fund (the “Fund”) (TSX: KEG.UN) today announced that its June 2025 distribution of $0.0946 per unit has been declared and is payable to unitholders of record as at June 21, 2025. The June 2025 distribution will be paid on June 30, 2025.

The Fund is a limited purpose, open-ended trust established under the laws of the Province of Ontario that, through The Keg Rights Limited Partnership, a subsidiary of the Fund, owns certain trademarks and other related intellectual property used by Keg Restaurants Ltd. (“KRL”). In exchange for use of those trademarks, KRL pays the Fund a royalty of 4% of gross sales of Keg restaurants included in the royalty pool.

With approximately 10,000 employees, over 100 restaurants and annual system sales exceeding $700 million, Vancouver-based KRL is the leading operator and franchisor of steakhouse restaurants in Canada and has a substantial presence in select regional markets in the United States. KRL continues to operate The Keg restaurant system and expand that system through the addition of both corporate and franchised Keg steakhouses. KRL has been named the number one restaurant company to work for in Canada in the latest edition of Forbes "Canada's Best Employers 2025" survey.

For further information contact:

Investor Relations

Telephone: 604-276-0242

E-mail: InvestorRelations@kegrestaurants.com

https://www.thekeg.com/en/keg-income-fund

data.title: Ventia Is Tuatahi's Primary Service Partner for Fiber Build, Connections, Maintenance
Western Region Gold to Issue Bonds of Up to 2 Billion Yuan; Shares Soar 10%
data.description:
Western Region Gold proposed to issue bonds amounting to 2 billion yuan.

The Chinese gold smelting company will issue medium-term bonds of up to 1 billion yuan and corporate bonds of the same amount, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The proceeds from the issuance will be used to repay debt, supplement working capital, and support project construction, the filing said.

The plan is still subject to shareholders' and regulators' approvals.

The company's shares soared 10% in recent trade.

data.title: Posiflex Technology's Chairman Steps Down
data.description:
Posiflex Technology's chairman, Chieh-Wen Cheng, resigned on Thursday, citing personal career planning as the reason for his departure.

The company has not appointed a successor yet, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Shares of the self-service technology solutions provider dropped over 3% recently.

data.title: Indian shares tumble at the open, tracking Asian peers, after Israel strikes Iran
data.description:
Indian equities fell at the open on Friday, tracking declines in Asian markets, after Israel launched military strikes on Iran, escalating tensions in the oil-rich Middle East.

The Nifty 50 was down 1.67% at 24,473 and the BSE Sensex slid 1.55% to 80,427.81, as of 9:15 a.m. IST.

All 13 sectors logged losses. The broader, more domestically focussed smallcaps and midcaps shed 2% each.

The MSCI Asia ex-Japan index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 1.1%, while safe havens like gold and the Swiss franc gained and oil prices <LCOc1> surged 9% amid fears of a supply disruption.

Israel said it struck Iranian nuclear targets to block Tehran from developing atomic weapons. The U.S. has ruled out any involvement in the strikes against Iran.

($1 = 85.5530 Indian rupees)

data.title: Ventia Has Been a Strategic Partner to New Zealand's Tuatahi Since 2011
Bermaz Auto Target Price Lowered to MYR0.86 from MYR0.95 by RHB IB >5248.KU
Grab Prices Upsized Offering of $1.5 Billion Bonds
data.description:
Singapore-based Grab upsized its offering of $1.25 billion worth of convertible senior bonds due 2030 to $1.5 billion, according to a company release on Tuesday.

Proceeds raised from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes, to optimize strategic flexibility and to fund potential acquisitions.

The bonds will mature on June 15, 2030, and will not bear a regular interest.
